title: Module 2: Fairness & Bias Evaluation Schema
description: Evaluates AI system fairness, bias detection, discrimination prevention, and compliance with fairness regulations for AI-based Accounting Information Systems

evaluation_criteria.criterion_name: Fairness Metrics & Special Category Data Compliance
evaluation_criteria.description: Validates fairness metrics compliance (demographic parity, equalized odds, disparate impact) and legal basis for processing special category data per GDPR Article 9
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Analyze fairness metrics across protected demographics, verify legal basis for special category data processing, review consent documentation
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Demographic parity within ±5%, disparate impact 0.8-1.25 range, equalized odds differences <10%, legal basis documented for special category data processing

required_fields.sample_size: minimum 1000 transactions per protected group for statistical significance

special_category_data_gdpr.applicability: Required if processing race, ethnic origin, health data, political opinions, etc.

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Statistical analysis reviewed by qualified statistician or data scientist, legal basis approved by DPO/legal counsel

thresholds.1.0: Parity difference ≤3%, statistically significant at p<0.05
thresholds.0.0: Parity difference >7% or not statistically tested
Disparate impact ratio <0.70 or >1.40 (four-fifths rule violation)

thresholds.1.0: Legal basis clearly documented for all special category data processing, explicit consent obtained where required, DPO/legal approval documented
thresholds.0.75: Legal basis documented, minor gaps in consent documentation
thresholds.0.5: Legal basis partially documented or consent procedures incomplete
thresholds.0.0: No legal basis documented for special category data processing (GDPR violation if applicable)

special_category_data_legal_basis.applicability: Scored N/A if no special category data processed; full weight if processing race, ethnicity, health, etc.

geographic_exemption.description: Special category data requirements apply if processing EU resident data
geographic_exemption.exemption_documentation: Legal opinion confirming no special category data or geographic scope

evaluation_criteria.description: Assesses ongoing bias monitoring system including frequency, alerting mechanisms, response procedures, and monitoring effectiveness
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review bias monitoring logs, alert configurations, response time metrics, remediation tracking
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Monthly minimum monitoring frequency, automated alerts configured, alert response time <72 hours, remediation tracked for all bias incidents

required_fields.monitoring_scope: All production AI systems in financial processes

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Monitoring procedures approved by AI Governance Lead, alert thresholds reviewed quarterly

thresholds.0.5: Monthly monitoring with occasional gaps (1-2 missed months)
thresholds.0.0: Less than monthly or frequent gaps

thresholds.1.0: Automated alerts configured for all fairness metrics, tested within 6 months
thresholds.0.75: Automated alerts configured, not recently tested

thresholds.1.0: Average response time <48 hours, 100% of alerts responded to within 72 hours
thresholds.0.75: Average response time <72 hours, >90% within 72 hours
thresholds.0.0: Response time >1 week or alerts not tracked

thresholds.1.0: All bias incidents tracked, remediation plans documented, implementation verified

evaluation_criteria.description: Evaluates documented bias mitigation procedures and their implementation including rebalancing, constraints, adversarial debiasing
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review bias mitigation documentation, implementation evidence, effectiveness testing results
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Documented mitigation procedures exist, at least 2 mitigation techniques implemented, effectiveness validated through testing, training data representativeness within ±10% of population distribution

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Mitigation procedures approved by AI Ethics Committee or equivalent, effectiveness testing reviewed by data science lead

thresholds.1.0: Comprehensive procedures covering multiple mitigation techniques, approved within 12 months
thresholds.0.75: Procedures documented and approved, covering 2+ techniques

thresholds.1.0: All protected groups within ±5% of population distribution
thresholds.0.75: All protected groups within ±10% of population distribution
thresholds.0.0: Significant representativeness gaps (>15%) or not measured

thresholds.1.0: Effectiveness validated through A/B testing, ≥50% bias reduction demonstrated

evaluation_criteria.description: Validates opt-out mechanisms and human review availability for AI-driven automated decisions with legal or similarly significant effects on individuals
evaluation_criteria.check_method: Review opt-out procedures, human review request logs, alternative decision-making processes, consumer notification of automated decision use
evaluation_criteria.pass_criteria: Opt-out mechanism documented and operational, human review available within 5 business days, alternative non-AI decision path exists, consumers notified of automated decision-making use in privacy policy

evidence_required.approval_requirements: Opt-out procedure approved by DPO/legal counsel, human review process tested within 12 months
evidence_required.applicability: Required if AI makes decisions with legal or similarly significant effects on EU residents or California residents

thresholds.1.0: Opt-out mechanism clearly documented, operational, tested within 12 months, accessible to consumers
thresholds.0.75: Mechanism documented and operational, not recently tested
thresholds.0.5: Mechanism exists but accessibility or documentation gaps

thresholds.1.0: Human review available within 48 hours, reviewers trained, review quality verified
thresholds.0.75: Human review available within 5 business days, basic training
thresholds.0.5: Human review available but delays common (>5 days)
thresholds.0.0: No human review available or excessive delays (>10 days)

thresholds.1.0: Privacy policy clearly discloses automated decision-making, logic/significance explained in plain language, opt-out instructions prominent
Alternative non-AI decision process documented, available, equivalent outcomes possible

geographic_exemption.description: Criterion scored N/A if AI does not make decisions with legal/significant effects OR not processing EU/California resident data
geographic_exemption.exemption_documentation: Legal assessment of decision significance + geographic scope documentation

severity_mapping.score_0.0_to_0.5: Material Weakness (if applicable to EU/California residents)
severity_mapping.score_NA: Not Applicable (no legally significant automated decisions or geographic exemption documented)

aggregate_scoring.calculation: sum(criterion_score × weight), excluding N/A criteria and renormalizing weights
aggregate_scoring.note: F2.4 scored N/A if not applicable; weights renormalized. F2.1 special category data component scored N/A if no special category data processed.

nist_ai_rmf.fairness_characteristic: All criteria align with Fairness characteristic

eu_ai_act.article_10: F2.1, F2.3 - Data governance for training datasets
eu_ai_act.article_9: F2.2 - Risk management system for bias

gdpr.article_9: F2.1 - Processing of special category data (race, ethnicity, health, etc.)
gdpr.recital_71: F2.1, F2.3 - Prohibition of discriminatory effects
gdpr.article_22: F2.4 - Right not to be subject to solely automated decision-making
gdpr.articles_13_14: F2.4 - Right to information about automated decision-making logic

ccpa.section_1798_125: F2.4 - Non-discrimination (cannot deny services for exercising CCPA rights)
ccpa.section_1798_185: F2.4 - Automated decision-making regulations, opt-out rights

fair_lending_laws.ecoa: F2.1 - Equal Credit Opportunity Act compliance
fair_lending_laws.fcra: F2.1 - Fair Credit Reporting Act adverse action requirements

interdependency_triggers.action: Analyze whether accuracy varies across demographic groups to distinguish 'fair but uniformly inaccurate' from 'accurate but biased'
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Distinguishes root cause: bias mitigation needed vs. model retraining needed. Determines appropriate remediation approach.

interdependency_triggers.action: Investigate governance root cause: Does privacy policy require opt-out mechanisms? Is there governance oversight of automated decision-making?
interdependency_triggers.rationale: Opt-out failures often indicate governance gap in privacy compliance framework

council_of_experts_integration.stage_1_domain_score: Aggregate score from F2.1-F2.4 using weighted average

stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m1_governance: Does governance framework mandate bias detection and fairness policies? Are accountability owners assigned?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m3_security: Do access controls suggest differential data access across demographics (proxy for bias risk)?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m4_explainability: Are explanations produced equally across demographic groups? Can disadvantaged groups obtain adequate explanation?
stage_2_peer_review_lens.review_by_m5_accuracy: Does accuracy vary systematically across demographic groups? Is model more error-prone for protected classes?

council_of_experts_integration.stage_3_arbitrator_inputs: M2 domain score + 4 peer review cards + fairness trigger log
